Now, if I want to replicate this:

- Need white strip at row 28 (1 row below gray box)
- Need player at rows 29-31 (below the white strip)
But the player is currently at rows 33-35. If I drop the item, the strip is at row 32, and the player is at rows 33-35. The strip is 4 rows below the gray box.
I need to get the player to rows 29-31 so the strip drops at row 28. But I can only move UP when carrying, and the carried item is blocking upward movement.
Hmm, what if I drop the item, then try to move the player UP without carrying? Earlier I tried UP without carrying and it was blocked. But maybe that was because I was at a different position.
Actually wait, let me re-examine. When I dropped the item at the right position (rows 32-36 carried, player at 37-39), I tried UP and it was blocked. But now I'm at rows 33-35 carrying. If I drop and try UP...
Actually, I already tried UP without carrying earlier and it was blocked (board_changed: false). So the player can't move UP without carrying.
OK so the only way to move UP is while carrying. But the carried item blocks upward movement.
Let me think about this from a different angle. What if the goal isn't to collect the gray boxes at all? What if the goal is to place the carried white block somewhere?
Looking at the center blue bar (rows 28-31, cols 28-39 with gray interior at rows 29-30), maybe I need to place the white block there?
Or maybe the goal is to bring the carried item to the left gray box?
Let me try a completely different approach: navigate to the left gray box and see what happens.
